common.title: When does this war end?
common.subtitle: A market-implied probability for the end of the Russia–Ukraine war, surrounded by the measured data moving the curve. No prediction is authored here — only what markets and indicators say, as of this moment.
common.footer: Non-commercial · Built in Kharkiv · CC BY 4.0

hero.label: Probability war has ended by date
hero.chartAria: Cumulative probability that the war has ended, by date
hero.caption: Liquidity-weighted across listed markets · monotone PCHIP interpolation
hero.medianNote: 50% probability crossing of the consensus CDF

history.heading: The war in data
history.scrubAria: Scrub the timeline to a date
history.scrubIntro: Drag the handle to scrub through measured indicators since 24 February 2022. Every figure shown is a snapshot from a free, open source.
history.lossNote: Personnel losses are not shown — no neutral, symmetric source exists; any figure would be a belligerent claim.

ground.heading: Today on the ground
ground.fireActivity: VIIRS fire/heat detections over Ukraine, last 5 days · NASA FIRMS
ground.intensityNote: GDELT volume intensity: the share of monitored global news coverage matching the war query, latest day. Not a count.

brief.sectionIntro: A short read on the week — what the data moved, and the news coverage behind the numbers.
brief.disclaimer: We don't write this. The summary, and the selection and translation of the news, are generated by AI and published automatically — without human editing. It reflects the data and coverage, not the views of this site, and can be wrong.
brief.heading: Daily brief

news.heading: Related news
news.via: Selected from GDELT coverage; titles translated automatically.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.lede: This page does not exist, or it has moved. Everything — the probability curve, the indicators, and the briefs — is reachable from the home page.

site.blurb: A non-commercial, Ukrainian-built project. Built in Kharkiv by {ITSUA}.
site.openData: CC BY 4.0 · open data, open source
